name: Q7694051
description: American jazz tenor saxophonist and arranger (1908-1999)
abstract: Teddy McRae (January 22, 1908 – March 4, 1999) was an American jazz tenor saxophonist and arranger.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: they sent Teddy McRae and a bunch of musicians from the Armstrong band over to the club where I worked... 'I'd be happy to.' ... 'we'd like an we will send for you when we get to New York.'

about_quotes.speaker: William Randolph "Cozy" Cole
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Teddy McRae replacing Chu Berry; the interviewee (Cozy Cole) does not speak about a personal or professional relationship with McRae in the provided text.

about_quotes.speaker: Happy Caldwell
about_quotes.evidence: Like the McRae brothers, they brought a terrific band from Philadelphia years ago. ... Teddy, Dave, all of them.
